mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output, which drives corporate earnings growth and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between INDPRO 6-month change and forward 60-day return falls below 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in consumer price inflation above 2.5% year-over-year triggers expectations of monetary tightening, which depresses equity valuations over the following week.
failure_condition: After trigger, average forward 5-day return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05) over all historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in industrial production and retail sales confirms broad economic momentum, reinforcing earnings optimism and pushing equity prices higher over the next month.
failure_condition: In periods where both conditions hold, the proportion of positive forward 20-day returns is less than 60%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate declines rapidly, it signals a tightening labour market that boosts wage growth and consumer confidence, generating positive equity returns within a week.
failure_condition: Mean forward 5-day return during trigger periods is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1,200,000 units per month reflect robust residential construction activity, which stimulates broader economic demand and lifts equity prices over the following month.
failure_condition: After threshold crossings, the average forward 20-day return is not positive (p > 0.05) or rank IC remains below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent low interest rates combined with rising inflation create negative real yields, which incentivizes capital misallocation and ultimately depresses equity performance over a quarter.
failure_condition: In months satisfying both conditions, the proportion of positive forward 60-day returns exceeds 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payrolls reinforces consumer spending and corporate profitability, directly supporting equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between PAYEMS 3-month change and forward 20-day return is less than 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a zero-interest-rate policy regime, the extraordinarily low discount rates inflate equity valuations and encourage risk-taking, producing positive forward returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: During periods when the trigger is active, the mean forward 60-day return is not positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).
